subject: Gaming
title: Parasocial Relationships with Game Characters
description: A fast breakdown of Parasocial Relationships with Game Characters for builders.

hook.text: You cried when a game character died. You formed a bond with someone who doesn't exist. That's parasocial.

buildup.text: Parasocial relationships are one-sided emotional connections with fictional or media characters.

discovery.text: RPG companions like Garrus, Tali, and Elizabeth feel like real friends after dozens of hours together.

twist.text: These bonds are psychologically real. Brain scans show similar activity to actual social connection.

climax.text: Gaming creates the deepest parasocial bonds in media because you interact, not just watch.

punchline.text: The character isn't real. But your connection to them is.

quiz.question: Why do games create stronger parasocial bonds than movies?
options: Because players interact with characters rather than passively watching
